nation: Première Nation des Innus de Nutashkuan
one_time_description: Gain sur règlement de dettes (gain on debt settlement)
economic_notes: Community receives Hydro-Quebec royalties from Romaine-2 project (Nanemessu agreement). GBEs include Placements Innus-Nutashkuan S.E.C. (holds stakes in Construction Atik S.E.C. 51% and 9286-1020 Québec inc. 51%) and Produits Forestiers Innus S.E.C. (51%). Fisheries (Pêcheries Nutashkuan) and outfitter (Pourvoirie Hipou) operations. Note 19: 64% of revenues from SAC and Health Canada. Financial statements are modified/restated from original August 2024 report.
notes: This is a MODIFIED/RESTATED financial statement (Rapport financier modifié) for fiscal year ended March 31, 2023, audited January 7, 2025. Original report dated August 1, 2024 had a disclaimer of opinion; revised to unqualified after new evidence obtained. Multiple restatement adjustments in Note 2 including write-off of employee advances ($602,631), addition of sand billing receivable, reclassification of capital items to lease expense, and deferred revenue adjustments. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Placements Innus-Nutashkuan S.E.C. (100%) and Produits Forestiers Innus S.E.C. (51%). Contingent liabilities: Produits Forestiers Innus litigation ($1,094,667 receivable + $592,000 claim); Construction Atik lawsuit of $2,414,606. No remuneration schedule included in provided text. isc_funding calculated from Note 11 SAC financing received ($25,072,986); total_government_transfers includes SAC, Health Canada, SCHL, SAA, Société Ishkuteu, MTQ, MPO, Justice, APNQL, Petapan, Forestry, Agriculture, Parks Canada, Québec en forme, CSSPNQL, Institut Tshakapesh, special child allowances. osr calculated as total revenue minus estimated government transfers.
